data.title: Tobacco smoke signals: Investors may benefit from tariff environment while consumers lose out
data.description:
The tobacco sector is impacted by tariffs due to the higher costs for imported tobacco products such as cigars and other products. President Trump's recent tariffs imposed a 10% baseline rate on imports from countries like the Dominican Republic and Honduras, while Nicaragua faces an 18% tariff. 

In general, higher tariff-related costs have been successfully passed on to consumers through price increases for products like cigars and accessories, which has left the tobacco giants relatively unscathed. Reports indicate that may be the case with the most recent tariff actions by the U.S., as higher prices on tobacco products in the U.S. will reportedly be seen within weeks.

Looking further ahead, analysts have warned that companies importing raw tobacco or finished products that face increased operational tariff-related expenses could reach a limit in passing on the extra costs to consumers and see demand trickle lower. 

Over the last six weeks, Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) scratched out a 0.3% gain and Imperial Brands (OTCQX:IMBBY) is 1.8% higher, while Altria (NYSE:MO) is down 2.8% and British American Tobacco (NYSE:BTI) is 1.1% lower. All four tobacco stocks have outperformed the broad market as investors have sought out defensive consumer staples favorites.

data.title: WD-40 raises fiscal 2025 EPS guidance to $5.25-$5.55 with gross margin anticipated at 55%-56%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: WD-40 Company (WDFC) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Steve Brass announced Q2 net sales of $146.1 million, a 5% increase from the prior fiscal year quarter. Adjusted for foreign currency impacts, net sales would have been $150.9 million, reflecting a 9% increase. Core maintenance product sales reached $139.3 million, a 6% rise driven by strong volume growth, particularly in EIMEA.
  • Sales in the Americas rose 3% to $65.5 million, bolstered by a 47% increase in WD-40 Multi-Use Product sales in Latin America due to a successful direct market transition in Brazil.
  • EIMEA sales grew 10% to $59.6 million, with significant volume growth in countries like Italy, France, and the Benelux region. Sales of WD-40 Specialist products in EIMEA increased 12%.
  • Asia Pacific sales declined by 1% to $21 million, primarily due to decreased sales in distributor markets affected by foreign currency dynamics. However, China saw a 5% increase in WD-40 Multi-Use Product sales.
  • CFO Sara Hyzer highlighted gross margin improvement to 54.6%, up 220 basis points from the prior year. This was attributed to lower costs of cans and specialty chemicals.
  • The company reiterated its commitment to divesting the home care and cleaning business and announced progress in discussions with potential buyers.

Outlook

  • Fiscal 2025 net sales are projected to range between $600 million and $630 million, with an anticipated gross margin of 55%-56%. This represents an upward revision from previous guidance.
  • Non-GAAP EPS guidance for fiscal 2025 has been raised to $5.25-$5.55, supported by a projected income tax rate of 22.5%.
  • Management highlighted a focus on achieving over 10% annual growth in premiumized product sales and over 15% compound annual growth for WD-40 Specialist net sales.

Financial Results

  • Gross margin for Q2 improved to 54.6%, driven by cost efficiencies in materials and manufacturing.
  • Adjusted EBITDA grew to $25.8 million, reflecting a 10% year-over-year increase. Operating income reached $23.3 million, up 11%.
  • Diluted EPS for Q2 was $2.19, including a one-time favorable tax adjustment of $0.87. Non-GAAP adjusted EPS was $1.32, a 16% increase from the prior year.

Q&A

  • Daniel Rizzo, Jefferies: Questioned tariff impacts and supply chain optimization. CEO Brass explained that the decentralized supply chain mitigates tariff risks and highlighted ongoing global cost optimizations.
  • Rizzo further inquired about gross margin dynamics. CFO Hyzer noted that while the current trajectory is strong, potential fluctuations in logistics costs and product mix could influence future margins.
  • Linda Bolton-Weiser, D.A. Davidson: Asked about SG&A expense increases. CFO Hyzer attributed the rise to higher employee-related expenses, incentive compensation, and increased brand-building investments.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about foreign exchange headwinds and SG&A cost increases, reflecting a slightly cautious tone.
  • Management maintained confidence in gross margin expansion and strategic initiatives, using phrases like "we are confident" and "we believe" to reinforce positive sentiment.
  • Compared to Q1 2025, management’s tone remained consistent in its optimism, while analysts appeared slightly more focused on potential risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Sales growth slowed from Q1's 9% increase to Q2's 5%, partly due to timing effects and foreign exchange headwinds in Asia Pacific.
  • Gross margin slightly declined from Q1's 54.8% but remains on track to exceed 55% by year-end 2025.
  • Analysts in Q2 concentrated more on tariff impacts and foreign currency challenges compared to Q1’s focus on promotional activities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Foreign exchange rate volatility remains a challenge, particularly in Asia Pacific and the Americas.
  • Tariff-related uncertainties could impact costs, though management has implemented supply chain optimizations to mitigate effects.
  • Delays in divesting the home care and cleaning business could temporarily affect financial metrics.

Final Takeaway

WD-40 Company's Q2 2025 earnings call highlighted solid progress in gross margin recovery, strategic market expansions, and premium product growth. With raised fiscal 2025 guidance for EPS and gross margin, the management remains optimistic about achieving long-term growth objectives despite currency and cost headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BP pauses project to make clean jet fuel at Spanish plant - Bloomberg
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) is pausing the expansion of a biofuels project to make sustainable aviation fuel at its Castellon oil refinery in Spain due to weaker than expected growth in the market, Bloomberg reported Tuesday.

The plan to make sustainable aviation fuel was part of a €2B decarbonization initiative at the site on Spain's east coast, outlined in 2023.

BP (NYSE:BP) will continue to consider a biofuels expansion at its Rotterdam plant, with the financial investment decision in the plant due by 2027, according to the report, even as it trims spending in low-carbon energy as part of a strategic reset.

A BP (BP) spokesperson declined to comment specifically on the Castellon project to Bloomberg, but it reiterated previous guidance that it might take the final investment decision on one new biofuels plant before 2027 depending on the economics.

The business of making cleaner aviation fuel has faced headwinds in recent months, and BP (BP) - which once had planned to build five new biofuels sites - has shelved plans for new plants in Germany and the U.S. while also pausing a project in Australia.

data.title: Crypto firm Ripple agrees to buy prime brokerage Hidden Road for $1.25B
data.description:
Ripple Labs, the company behind the XRP (XRP-USD) cryptocurrency, agreed to buy multi-asset prime brokerage firm Hidden Road for $1.25B, a deal that's expected to bolster Ripple's efforts on the stablecoin front, it said on Tuesday.

Hidden Road offers institutions clearing, prime brokerage and financing across foreign exchange, digital assets, swaps and fixed income. It clears $3T annually across markets with over 300 clients. Ripple participated in Hidden Road's Series B funding round and is a customer of its platform.

With the acquisition, which is expected to close in the months ahead, Hidden Road will use Ripple's dollar-pegged stablecoin, RLUSD, as collateral across its prime brokerage products. This, in turn, enables efficient cross-margining between traditional and digital asset markets -- the likes of which haven't been seen before, Ripple said in a statement.

In addition, Hidden Road will migrate its post-trade activity across the XRP ledger to reduce costs and streamline operations. 

Through its payments solution, Ripple is aiming to enhance cost efficiency and liquidity for cross-border transactions while offering secure, bank-grade digital asset custody services to Hidden Road's clients.

Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.

Meanwhile, in Tuesday morning trading, XRP (XRP-USD) advanced 2.6% over the last 24 hours to $1.95 per token, amid a broader risk-on day.

data.title: Amazon's latest AI voice model demonstrates deeper understanding of conversations
data.description:
Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) latest artificial intelligence model, Nova Sonic, demonstrates an in-depth understanding of conversations, identifying more complex elements such as tone, inflection and pacing, the company said today.

Amazon shares jumped 4.5% during early market trading on Tuesday.

Nova Sonic unifies speech understanding and speech generation into a single model, which improves and simplifies the development of voice applications such as service call automation and AI agents.

"Traditional approaches in building voice-enabled applications involve complex orchestration of multiple models, such as speech recognition to convert speech to text, large language models to understand and generate responses, and text-to-speech to convert text back to audio," Amazon said. 

This fragmented approach makes development more complex and tends to lose context such as tone. It's an issue Amazon said it has solved through the single model approach.

"This unification enables the model to adapt the generated voice response to the acoustic context (e.g., tone, style) and the spoken input, resulting in more natural dialogue," Amazon said. "Nova Sonic even understands the nuances of human conversation, including the speaker's natural pauses and hesitations, waiting to speak until the appropriate time, and gracefully handling barge-ins."

Nova Sonic is available through a new API in Amazon Bedrock.

data.title: Ross Stores gains after Wells Fargo calls it a defensive standout amid crumbling economic data
data.description:
Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST) shot up 3.7% in early trading on Tuesday after Wells Fargo singled out the retail stock as a top pick for a defensive basket amid the global trade war.

The firm sees Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST) as part of a select few companies in the retail sector that feature less economically sensitive brand models, as well as downside support and healthy free cash flow.

Ross Stores (ROST) was upgraded by Wells Fargo to an Overweight rating due to its more defensive nature, downside support on valuation, and the levers in place to sustain/beat current Street forecasts. 

"While ROST is clearly dealing with more volatility than is typical for their model, we see today's valuation as near-trough and would also expect a much better relative performance in a slower macro tape," updated analyst Ike Bochurow.

Wells Fargo raised its price target on Ross Stores (ROST) to $150 from $140.

data.title: Janus Henderson inks pact to manage $45B of insurer's money
data.description:
Janus Henderson (NYSE:JHG) stock jumped 8.2% in Tuesday trading after it announced a deal with insurer Guardian Life to manage its bond portfolio, making it the latest investment firm to increase its business by managing insurance funds.

The pact gives JHG a mandate to manage a $45B bond portfolio for Guardian’s general account, which will include investment grade corporates and securitized credit, the companies said Tuesday.

In recent years, more insurance companies have been turning to asset managers to manage the money they collect in premiums, with the aim that investment firms can achieve higher returns than insurers' in-house teams can.

Guardian will also provide up to $400M of seed capital to help accelerate Janus Henderson’s (NYSE:JHG) innovation in securitized credit and high quality active fixed income products, as well as other leading fixed income capabilities.

In exchange, Guardian will get equity warrants and other economic consideration "designed to support a shared goal of accelerating growth and driving value creation," the companies said. The Wall Street Journal reported that Guardian will get 1.6M warrants on JHG stock, citing a person familiar with the transaction.

The two companies will also co-develop proprietary, multi-asset solution model portfolios for Guardian’s dually registered broker-dealer and registered investment advisor, Park Avenue Securities, which has over 2,400 advisors covering ~$58.5B of client assets under management.

"This multifaceted, innovative partnership, founded on a shared set of client-focused values, leverages our complementary strengths, creates alignment for mutual growth, and intends to achieve mutually beneficial outcomes for policyholders, our clients, shareholders, and employees," said Janus Henderson (JHG) CEO Ali Dibadj.

As part of the agreement, Guardian investment professionals currently supporting the in-scope asset classes will have the opportunity to join Janus Henderson (JHG), providing continuity in the management of these assets while enhancing JHG’s specialized insurance investment capabilities.

The partnership is expected to close at the end of Q2 2025.

data.title: Microsoft, Google AI deals in crosshairs of Sens. Warren, Wyden: report
data.description:
• The respective cloud computing deals that Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) have signed with artificial intelligence companies have come under fire from two Democratic lawmakers, who are concerned they could impact competition.
  • “We are concerned that corporate partnerships within the AI sector discourage competition, circumvent our antitrust laws, and result in fewer choices and higher prices for businesses and consumers using AI tools,” Senators Elizabeth Warren and Ron Wyden wrote in a pair of letters, according to Reuters. 
  • The senators highlighted the deals that Microsoft and Google have signed with OpenAI, Anthropic and others as examples.
  • The letters also seek to understand how much the AI companies have paid the cloud service providers, whether they have exclusive rights to license the AI models and if there are plans for the publicly traded giants to acquire their AI partners.
  • Microsoft has made a multi-billion dollar financial investment into OpenAI, while Google recently invested another $1B into Anthropic.
  • Microsoft and Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: Health insurers stocks, AVGO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Tuesday, finding stability after a bout of volatility triggered by President Trump’s tariff announcement, amid signs of easing tensions and optimism around potential trade negotiations with certain nations.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Humana (NYSE:HUM) +12% – Shares of major health insurers surged after the U.S. Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) finalized a significant increase in 2025 Medicare Advantage payments. The agency confirmed a 5.06% average rate hike—well above the 2.23% initially proposed—marking a 2.83-point upward revision, which could amount to over $25B in additional industry funding. Alignment Healthcare (NASDAQ:ALHC) gained 11%, Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) and UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) each rose 6%, while Clover Health (NASDAQ:CLOV) and CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) rose 6.5%, and Centene (NYSE:CNC) rose 3.5% on the news.
  • Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) +3% – The chipmaker’s stock advanced after announcing a share repurchase program of up to $10B, set to run through December 31, 2025. The company noted that buybacks could occur through various methods, including open market or privately negotiated transactions. CFO Kirsten Spears said the move underscores the Board’s confidence in Broadcom’s robust cash flow and commitment to returning value to shareholders.
  • Levi Strauss (NYSE:LEVI) +10% – The stock surged after the company delivered mixed Q1 results, surpassing profit expectations. Revenue rose 3% Y/Y (up 9% on an organic basis), with the Levi’s brand growing 8% globally. Gross margin improved to 62.1% from 58.8%, while adjusted EBIT margin jumped 400 basis points to 13.4%. The company reaffirmed full-year revenue guidance of -1% to -2% and slightly raised the midpoint of its EPS outlook to $1.225 (consensus $1.22). CEO Michelle Gass highlighted Levi's global presence, strong margins, and supply chain agility as key strengths amid ongoing uncertainty. The company maintained its long-term target of reaching $10B in revenue and a 15% operating margin.
  • CorMedix (CRMD) +20% – The stock surged after the company projected preliminary Q1 revenue of $39M, beating the $32.2M consensus, with adjusted EBITDA expected to exceed $22.5M. The upside came from stronger-than-anticipated orders in late March and improved insight into customer inventory levels. CorMedix raised its H1 2025 sales outlook for existing customers to $62M–$70M. While Q2 sales are expected to be lower than Q1 due to shipment timing, the company emphasized this does not reflect weaker patient demand and maintains confidence in utilization trends for the rest of the year.

Biggest stock losers

  • RPM International (RPM) -5% – Shares declined after the building materials company reported lower-than-expected FQ3 results with a 3% Y/Y drop in revenue, citing poor weather, FX headwinds, and transition costs from plant consolidation. “Unseasonably cold weather in the South and wildfires in the West dampened demand in key regions,” said CEO Frank C. Sullivan. Looking ahead, the company expects flat sales in Q4 FY25, with adjusted EBIT growth in the low single digits amid ongoing macro challenges.

data.title: 15 stocks that offer value and dividends as tariff news spooks investors
data.description:
The latest market selloff has sparked fear among investors, with Wall Street's broader market averages tumbling since U.S. President Donald Trump's sweeping reciprocal tariffs. 

While markets slide, two safe havens traders may look to turn to are value and dividends. 

Below is a curated list of the top 15 U.S. stocks with a market capitalization of at least $10B that offer a dividend yield of 3% or greater along with a Seeking Alpha quant factor valuation rating of an A- to an A+. List is ranked by SA quant grades.

No. 15: Dow Inc (NYSE:DOW), quant grade of 2.83.

No. 14: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE), quant grade of 2.89.

No. 13: LyondellBasell Industries (NYSE:LYB), quant grade of 2.91.

No. 12: Best Buy (NYSE:BBY), quant grade of 2.93.

No. 11: Archer-Daniels-Midland Company (NYSE:ADM), quant grade of 2.99.

No. 10: HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ), quant grade of 3.07. 

No. 9: Target (NYSE:TGT), quant grade of 3.09.

No. 8: United Parcel Services (NYSE:UPS), quant grade of 3.12.

No. 7: The Kraft Heinz Company (NASDAQ:KHC), quant grade of 3.16.

No. 6: Edison International (NYSE:EIX), quant grade of 3.23. 

No. 5: Ares Capital (NASDAQ:ARCC), quant grade of 3.24.

No. 4: Ford Motor Company (NYSE:F), quant grade of 3.32.

No. 3: Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), quant grade of 3.40.

No. 2: Altria Group (NYSE:MO), quant grade of 3.45. 

No. 1: Omega Healthcare Investors (NYSE:OHI), quant grade of 4.43. 

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

For further exposure towards dividends and value, investors can turn towards exchange traded funds for further diversification. 

Dividend ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VIG), (NYSEARCA:SCHD), (NYSEARCA:VYM), (NYSEARCA:DGRO), (NYSEARCA:SDY), (NASDAQ:DVY), (NASDAQ:DGRW), (BATS:NOBL), (NYSEARCA:HDV), (NYSEARCA:FVD), (NASDAQ:RDVY), (NASDAQ:VIGI), and (NASDAQ:SDVY).

Value ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VTV), (NYSEARCA:VBR), (BATS:EFV), (NYSEARCA:CGDV), (NYSEARCA:AVUV), (NYSEARCA:IWS), (NYSEARCA:DFUV), (NYSEARCA:DFAT), (NYSEARCA:DFIV), (BATS:QUAL), (NYSEARCA:AVDV), and (NYSEARCA:IJJ).
